description: Fallback for other fetch directives. If a specific directive is absent, the browser uses default-src.
notes: Set as a secure baseline; override with more permissive rules per specific directive.

description: Controls valid sources for JavaScript. The single most important CSP directive for preventing XSS.
notes: 'unsafe-inline' and 'unsafe-eval' should be avoided. Use nonces or hashes instead.

description: Controls valid sources for CSS stylesheets, including inline <style> blocks.
notes: 'unsafe-inline' allows inline styles; prefer nonces or hashes.

description: Controls valid sources for images, including favicons.
notes: data: URIs allow inline images; restrict to trusted hosts where possible.

description: Restricts URLs that can be loaded using fetch(), XHR, WebSockets, and EventSource.
notes: Critical for preventing data exfiltration via AJAX to attacker-controlled endpoints.

description: Controls valid sources for fonts loaded with @font-face.
notes: Google Fonts requires both font-src and style-src allowlisting.

description: Controls valid sources for <object>, <embed>, and <applet> elements.
notes: Should almost always be set to 'none' — these elements are vectors for plugin-based attacks.

description: Controls valid sources for <audio> and <video> elements.
Controls valid sources for <iframe> and <frame> elements.
Controls valid sources for nested browsing contexts (frames) and Web Workers.
notes: Deprecated in favour of frame-src (frames) and worker-src (workers).

description: Controls valid sources for Worker, SharedWorker, and ServiceWorker scripts.
Controls valid sources for Web App Manifest files.
Controls valid sources for prefetch and prerender operations.
notes: Deprecated and removed from most browsers.

description: Restricts the URLs that can be used as the action of a <form> element.
notes: Not a fetch directive — does not fall back to default-src.

description: Restricts which URLs can embed the page in a frame. Replacement for the X-Frame-Options header.
notes: 'none' prevents all framing (equivalent to X-Frame-Options: DENY). Prevents clickjacking.

description: Restricts the URLs that can be used in a <base> element. Prevents base-tag injection attacks.
notes: Does not fall back to default-src. Often set to 'none' or 'self'.

description: Restricts the URLs to which a document can navigate by any means.
Specifies a URL where the browser sends violation reports when a CSP policy is violated.
notes: Deprecated in favour of report-to. Still widely supported as fallback.

description: Specifies a Reporting API endpoint group where violation reports should be sent.
notes: Requires a Report-To response header defining the endpoint group.

description: Instructs browsers to upgrade all HTTP URLs to HTTPS before fetching.
notes: Useful for migrating legacy sites to HTTPS; does not affect navigation.

description: Blocks all mixed content (HTTP resources on HTTPS pages), even in passive contexts like images.
notes: Deprecated in favour of upgrade-insecure-requests in most implementations.

description: Applies restrictions similar to the <iframe> sandbox attribute to the page: disables scripts, forms, popups, etc.
notes: With no values, maximally restrictive. Add individual allow-* tokens to loosen.
